# Python 3.11 project
uv venv --python 3.11
source .venv/bin/activate

# Python 3.12 project (different directory)
uv venv --python 3.12
source .venv/bin/activate

docs.title: ⚡ Why UV?
docs.text: Benefit Description Speed 10-100x faster than pip Smart Caching Reuses downloaded packages Better Resolution Handles dependency conflicts better Drop-in Replacement Same commands as pip Lock Files Create reproducible builds Python Versions Easy version management

docs.title: 🎯 Key Commands
docs.text: Task Command Create venv uv venv Install package uv pip install <package> Install from file uv pip install -r requirements.txt List packages uv pip list Save requirements uv pip freeze > requirements.txt Upgrade package uv pip install --upgrade <package> Uninstall package uv pip uninstall <package>

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text:
requirements.in (optional, recommended) - Loose version constraints - Human-maintained - Use with uv pip compile

requirements.txt (required) - Locked versions - Auto-generated - Used for installation

pyproject.toml (recommended) - Project metadata - Dependency definitions - Tool configurations

docs.title: Scripts
docs.text: Both setup scripts are fully automated and include: - Step-by-step progress indicators - Error handling - Complete project scaffolding - Example code and tests

docs.title: 💡 Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Always use virtual environments - Never install globally
  2. Lock your dependencies - Use uv pip freeze > requirements.txt
  3. Separate dev dependencies - Use pyproject.toml optional-dependencies
  4. Use specific Python versions - uv venv --python 3.12
  5. Alias for convenience - Add alias pip='uv pip' to bashrc

docs.title: UV not found after installation
docs.text:
# Add to PATH
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Or reinstall
pip install uv --break-system-packages

docs.title: Virtual environment won't activate
docs.text:
# Make sure you're using the correct command
source .venv/bin/activate  # Linux/Mac

# Check if venv exists
ls -la .venv/

docs.title: Package conflicts
docs.text:
# Let UV resolve dependencies
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Python Dependency Management: pip vs uv
docs.text: Why uv? uv brings more functionality than pip and it is just as easy to use. Here is a comparision of benefits.

docs.title: Creating a Virtual Environment
docs.text:
Traditional pip: 

python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate

With uv: 

uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
✨ Benefit: Simpler command, faster creation

docs.title: Installing Packages
docs.text:
Traditional pip: 

pip install requests pandas numpy
# Takes: ~30 seconds

With uv: 

uv pip install requests pandas numpy
# Takes: ~3 seconds ⚡
✨ Benefit: 10x faster installation

docs.title: Creating Requirements
docs.text:
Traditional pip: 

pip freeze > requirements.txt

With uv: 

uv pip freeze > requirements.txt
✨ Benefit: Same command, works identically

docs.title: Installing from Requirements
docs.text:
Traditional pip: 

pip install -r requirements.txt
# Takes: ~45 seconds for 50 packages

With uv: 

uv pip install -r requirements.txt
# Takes: ~5 seconds for 50 packages ⚡
✨ Benefit: Dramatically faster, especially for large projects

docs.title: Upgrading Packages
docs.text:
Traditional pip: 

pip install --upgrade requests

With uv: 

uv pip install --upgrade requests
✨ Benefit: Same command, faster execution

docs.title: Before (Traditional pip)
docs.text:
# Create project
mkdir myproject && cd myproject

# Create virtual environment (slow)
python3 -m venv .venv

# Activate
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install dependencies (slow)
pip install django djangorestframework psycopg2-binary

# Wait... ⏳ (30-60 seconds)

# Save requirements
pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Total time: ~60-90 seconds

docs.title: After (With uv)
docs.text:
# Create project
mkdir myproject && cd myproject

# Create virtual environment (fast)
uv venv

# Activate
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install dependencies (fast)
uv pip install django djangorestframework psycopg2-binary

# Done! ✨ (5-10 seconds)

# Save requirements
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Total time: ~10-15 seconds ⚡

docs.title: Before (Traditional pip)
docs.text:
mkdir mcp-server && cd mcp-server
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install MCP and dependencies
pip install mcp httpx pydantic anyio starlette

# Wait for installation... ⏳

pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Create server.py
# ... your code ...

# Total setup time: ~45 seconds

docs.title: After (With uv)
docs.text:
mkdir mcp-server && cd mcp-server
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install MCP and dependencies
uv pip install mcp httpx pydantic anyio starlette

# Installation done! ⚡

uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Create server.py
# ... your code ...

# Total setup time: ~8 seconds

docs.title: Migration Guide: Switching from pip to uv
Step 1: Install uv
docs.text: pip install uv --break-system-packages

docs.title: Step 2: Replace pip with uv pip
docs.text:
Just prefix all your pip commands:

Old Command New Command pip install pkg uv pip install pkg pip list uv pip list pip freeze uv pip freeze pip uninstall pkg uv pip uninstall pkg

docs.title: Step 3: (Optional) Create alias
docs.text:
Add to your ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc: 

alias pip='uv pip'

Now you can use pip install and it will use uv automatically! ⚡

docs.title: Real-World Speed Comparison
docs.text:
Based on installing common data science stack:

# Packages: pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, matplotlib, seaborn
Tool Time Speed pip ~120 seconds 1x uv ~12 seconds 10x faster ⚡ 
# Packages: django, djangorestframework, celery, redis, pytest
Tool Time Speed pip ~90 seconds 1x uv ~8 seconds 11x faster ⚡

docs.title: Use pip when:
docs.text:
• You need maximum compatibility
  • Working on older systems
  • Required by organizational policy
  • Already have working scripts

docs.title: Use uv when:
docs.text:
• Speed matters (almost always!)
  • Setting up new projects
  • CI/CD pipelines
  • Large dependency trees
  • Working with multiple projects
  • Want better dependency resolution

docs.title: Best Practice: Use uv everywhere!
docs.text: It's a drop-in replacement that just works faster. 🚀

docs.title: Common Questions
docs.text:
Q: Will my existing requirements.txt work? A: Yes! 100% compatible.

Q: Can I mix pip and uv? A: Yes, but stick to one in each project for consistency.

Q: Does uv work with pyproject.toml? A: Yes! uv pip install -e . works perfectly.

Q: What about conda environments? A: uv works inside conda environments too!

Q: Is it production-ready? A: Yes! Used by many large projects and companies.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
✅ Drop-in replacement - Same commands as pip ⚡ 10-100x faster - Written in Rust 🎯 Better resolution - Handles conflicts better 💾 Smart caching - Reuses downloads 🔒 Lock files - Reproducible builds 🐍 Python versions - Easy version management 📦 Compatible - Works with all pip tools

Bottom line: Switch to uv and save hours of waiting! ⏱️→⚡

uv pip install mcp httpx pydantic

docs.title: Update Dependencies
docs.text:
uv pip list --outdated
uv pip install --upgrade requests
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

docs.title: Pro Tips
docs.text: ✨ Speed: 10-100x faster than pip 🔄 Cache: Automatic package caching 🎯 Compatible: Drop-in pip replacement 🔒 Lock files: Use uv pip compile for reproducible builds 🐍 Python versions: Specify with --python X.Y

docs.title: Common Errors & Fixes
docs.text: Error Solution "uv not found" pip install uv --break-system-packages Can't activate venv source .venv/bin/activate Package conflicts uv pip compile requirements.in then install Wrong Python version uv venv --python 3.12

docs.title: Why UV?
docs.text:
• ⚡ Extremely fast - Written in Rust
  • 🎯 Better dependency resolution - More reliable than pip
  • 💾 Smart caching - Reuses downloaded packages
  • 🔄 Drop-in replacement - Same commands as pip
  • 🛡️ Reliable - Handles conflicts better

docs.title: Remember
docs.text:
1. Always activate your venv: source .venv/bin/activate
  2. Save your deps: uv pip freeze > requirements.txt
  3. Use uv pip compile for locked dependencies
  4. Each project gets its own venv
  5. Prefix pip commands with uv: uv pip install

docs.title: Complete Guide to Using UV for Python Dependencies
docs.text: uv is an extremely fast Python package installer and resolver written in Rust. It's a drop-in replacement for pip that's 10-100x faster!

docs.title: On Ubuntu/Linux
docs.text:
# Install via pip
pip install uv --break-system-packages

# Or use the official installer (if available)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text: uv --version

docs.title: 1. Creating a Virtual Environment
docs.text:
# Create a new virtual environment
uv venv

# Create with a specific name
uv venv myenv

# Create with specific Python version
uv venv --python 3.12
uv venv --python 3.11

# Create in a specific directory
uv venv /path/to/venv

docs.title: 2. Activating Virtual Environments
docs.text:
# On Linux/Mac
source .venv/bin/activate

# Or if you named it differently
source myenv/bin/activate

# To deactivate
deactivate

docs.title: 3. Installing Packages
docs.text:
# Install a single package
uv pip install requests

# Install multiple packages
uv pip install requests numpy pandas

# Install specific version
uv pip install requests==2.31.0

# Install with version constraints
uv pip install "requests>=2.28.0,<3.0.0"

# Install from requirements.txt
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

# Install package in editable mode (for development)
uv pip install -e .

docs.title: 4. Package Management
docs.text:
# List installed packages
uv pip list

# Show package information
uv pip show requests

# Uninstall packages
uv pip uninstall requests

# Uninstall multiple packages
uv pip uninstall requests numpy pandas

# Upgrade a package
uv pip install --upgrade requests

# Upgrade all packages (use with caution)
uv pip install --upgrade $(uv pip list --format=freeze | cut -d= -f1)

docs.title: 5. Requirements Files
docs.text:
# Generate requirements.txt from current environment
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Install from requirements.txt
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

# Compile requirements with locked versions
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt

docs.title: Using pyproject.toml
docs.text:
Create a pyproject.toml file:

[project]
name = "my-project"
version = "0.1.0"
dependencies = [
    "requests>=2.28.0",
    "pandas>=2.0.0",
    "numpy>=1.24.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=7.0.0",
    "black>=23.0.0",
    "mypy>=1.0.0",
]

Then install:

# Install project dependencies
uv pip install -e .

# Install with dev dependencies
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Sync Dependencies
docs.text:
# Sync environment to match requirements exactly
uv pip sync requirements.txt

# This removes packages not in requirements.txt
# and installs/updates to match exactly

docs.title: Compile Dependencies
docs.text:
# Create requirements.in with loose constraints
echo "requests>=2.28" > requirements.in
echo "pandas>=2.0" >> requirements.in

# Compile to locked requirements.txt
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt

# This creates a fully locked requirements.txt with all dependencies

docs.title: Example 1: New Python Project
docs.text:
# Create project directory
mkdir my-project
cd my-project

# Create virtual environment
uv venv

# Activate it
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install dependencies
uv pip install requests pandas numpy

# Save requirements
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Work on your project...

# Deactivate when done
deactivate

docs.title: Example 2: Clone and Setup Existing Project
docs.text:
# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/user/project.git
cd project

# Create virtual environment
uv venv

# Activate it
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install from requirements
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

# Or if using pyproject.toml
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Example 3: MCP Server Setup
docs.text:
# Create project directory
mkdir mcp-server
cd mcp-server

# Create virtual environment with Python 3.12
uv venv --python 3.12

# Activate
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install MCP dependencies
uv pip install mcp httpx pydantic

# Save requirements
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

# Create your server.py
# ... (your code here)

# When done
deactivate

docs.title: Example 4: Multiple Projects with Different Dependencies
docs.text:
# Project 1 - Data Science
mkdir data-project
cd data-project
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install pandas numpy scikit-learn matplotlib
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt
deactivate

# Project 2 - Web Development
cd ..
mkdir web-project
cd web-project
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install django djangorestframework
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt
deactivate

docs.title: 1. Always Use Virtual Environments
docs.text:
# Create venv for each project
uv venv

# Never install packages globally (avoid --break-system-packages)

docs.title: 2. Use Requirements Files
docs.text:
# Keep two files for better control:

# requirements.in (loose constraints)
requests>=2.28
pandas>=2.0

# requirements.txt (locked versions)
# Generated with: uv pip compile requirements.in
requests==2.31.0
pandas==2.1.4
# ... (all sub-dependencies locked)

docs.title: 3. Separate Dev Dependencies
docs.text:
# requirements.in
requests>=2.28

# requirements-dev.in
-c requirements.txt  # constrain to main requirements
pytest>=7.0
black>=23.0
mypy>=1.0

# Compile both
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt
uv pip compile requirements-dev.in -o requirements-dev.txt

docs.title: 4. Update Dependencies Safely
docs.text:
# Check for updates
uv pip list --outdated

# Update one package
uv pip install --upgrade requests

# Re-compile requirements
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt

docs.title: Comparison: pip vs uv
docs.text: Task pip uv Install package pip install requests uv pip install requests Install from file pip install -r requirements.txt uv pip install -r requirements.txt Create venv python -m venv .venv uv venv List packages pip list uv pip list Freeze deps pip freeze > requirements.txt uv pip freeze > requirements.txt Speed Normal 10-100x faster

docs.title: Issue: "uv: command not found"
docs.text:
# Reinstall uv
pip install uv --break-system-packages

# Or add to PATH if installed locally
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

docs.title: Issue: Virtual environment not activating
docs.text:
# Make sure you're using the correct activation command
source .venv/bin/activate  # Linux/Mac

# Check if venv was created
ls -la .venv/

docs.title: Issue: Package conflicts
docs.text:
# Use pip compile to resolve dependencies
uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt

# Then install from compiled requirements
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Issue: Need specific Python version
docs.text:
# Ensure Python version is installed first
sudo apt install python3.12

# Then create venv with that version
uv venv --python 3.12

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text:
# Environment Management
uv venv                          # Create virtual environment
source .venv/bin/activate        # Activate (Linux/Mac)
deactivate                       # Deactivate

# Package Installation
uv pip install <package>         # Install package
uv pip install -r requirements.txt  # Install from file
uv pip install -e .              # Install in editable mode

# Package Information
uv pip list                      # List installed packages
uv pip show <package>            # Show package info
uv pip freeze                    # Show all packages with versions

# Package Management
uv pip uninstall <package>       # Uninstall package
uv pip install --upgrade <pkg>   # Upgrade package

# Requirements Management
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt # Save current environment
uv pip compile req.in -o req.txt # Compile locked requirements
uv pip sync requirements.txt     # Sync to exact requirements

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• Official Documentation: https://github.com/astral-sh/uv
  • Performance Benchmarks: https://github.com/astral-sh/uv#benchmarks
  • Migration from pip: Most commands are drop-in replacements, just prefix with uv

docs.title: Pro Tips
docs.text:
1. Speed: uv is fastest when installing from a clean state
  2. Cache: uv automatically caches packages for even faster reinstalls
  3. Compatibility: Works with existing pip requirements.txt files
  4. Resolution: uv has better dependency resolution than pip
  5. Python versions: Use uv venv --python X.Y to specify Python version
